other_observers: Brandon Percival Brad Steger  Sophie Steger Mark Peterson Dan Maynard; Stan Oswald found this bird first on the same date but closer to Adobe Reservoir within one mile of where we found it, in the SE corner of the NE arm of the reservoir.
specific_location: near Adobe Creek Reservoir and visible from there, first on the northern part of Rd 11 and then flying to the north side of the countyline road, named A Rd in Kiowa Cnty and WW Rd in Bent County, between the 17 Rd. and 19 Rd., probably on the fenceline of what would be the 18 Rd. if, in fact there was a round there.
description: This big white large-headed bird without noticeable ear tufts was an owl. The facial disc was completely white. Its irises were bright yellow with large dark pupils. The crown was white with fine dark streaking. The wings back and breast had dark thick streaking on a white background. The legs appeared feathered. The feet were not visible.   The top of the wings, visible when it flew were white with dark streaks, the wings very broad, the wing beats slow, the wing tips very rounded.Behaviors: Perched on top of road cut and flew three times to fence posts. Its flight was always low often below the height of the barbed wire fence. When first spotted perched on a cut bank. it remained motionless until it became aware of our presence. It then spent much of its time looking at us.Call: none heardPlumage: Ist winter male possibly
